scope.summary: UI account rail that lists every logged-in account and switches the active one. Account state is managed frontend-side in the reactive session store (src/lib/session.svelte.ts): accounts are adopted from login/QR/restore AccountProfiles into a per-account bucket, RailNav renders one avatar per account with an active ring + unread badge, and switchAccount swaps the reactive view (conversations, threads, contacts, composer target) to the selected account's bucket. No new Rust command is needed — accounts come from the existing login/restore commands, so the originally-planned list_accounts/set_active_account backend pair was intentionally not built.
goals: session store: per-account buckets (Map<accountId, AccountData>); adoptAccount adds rail entries; switchAccount/activate swap the reactive view to the target account
RailNav: one avatar per account with active-ring + unread badge + an add-account button
Switching swaps the conversation list + active thread + composer target to the selected account
A non-secret account-switch diagnostic (account ids + conversation count only) is logged for observability
nonGoals: Rust list_accounts/set_active_account commands (state is frontend-managed from login/restore profiles)
Persisting the active-account selection across restarts

acceptance.description: Frontend builds with the account rail (avatar per account, active ring, unread badge, add-account button) and svelte-check is clean.
Frontend production build succeeds with the switcher store + RailNav.

Live: with two accounts logged in, clicking a rail avatar swaps the active account and its view; the non-secret core log shows 'account-switch: active <A> -> <B>'. Evidence captures only non-secret diagnostics.
